svg.text: Sudoku in Practice — Solvers, Generators, Extensions
• Norvig's Python solver — 50 LOC; canonical reference.
• Knuth's Dancing Links (DLX) exact-cover formulation — solves any Sudoku in ms.
• tdoku (Tom Dillon, 2019) — current speed leader using SIMD bitboards, ~0.5 µs / puzzle.
• fsss2 (Mladen Dobrichev) — older record holder via hand-tuned bitboard operations.
• Z3 / MiniSAT encoding — 729 booleans, ~10k clauses; solves but slower than dedicated.
• Genetic algorithms / simulated annealing — work for non-strict Sudoku variants.
• Newspaper apps (NYT, Times of London) embed solvers for hint generation.
• Generation: start from a solved grid, remove cells while uniqueness holds.
• Difficulty rating: classify by which propagation rules are needed (single → pair → X-wing → guess).
• Minimum clues: 17 — proven by McGuire et al. 2014 with 7-billion-CPU-hour exhaustive search.
• Variants: Killer Sudoku (cage-sum constraints), Samurai (5 overlapping grids),
16×16 (hex digits), Diagonal Sudoku (extra constraint), Jigsaw (irregular boxes).
• Sudoku is NP-complete on n × n boards (Yato & Seta, 2003) but tractable for fixed n=9.
A well-formed Sudoku has exactly one solution; uniqueness is the hardest invariant to verify.
